title: Context-Aware Timeout Handler
description: Implement context-aware task execution with per-task timeouts. Each line of input describes a task with its name, simulated duration in milliseconds, and deadline in milliseconds. Run each task concurrently using goroutines with context timeouts. A task that completes within its deadline outputs DONE; one that exceeds it outputs TIMEOUT. Return results sorted alphabetically by task name.
constraints: Each input line is space-separated: task_name duration_ms deadline_ms
Use context.WithTimeout to enforce per-task deadlines
A task DONE if duration_ms < deadline_ms (simulate work with time.Sleep or channel select)
A task TIMEOUT if duration_ms >= deadline_ms
Return results sorted alphabetically by task name
Output format: 'task_name DONE' or 'task_name TIMEOUT' (one per line)

examples.output:
task-a DONE
task-b TIMEOUT
task-c DONE
examples.explanation: task-a (100ms) finishes before its 200ms deadline: DONE. task-b (300ms) exceeds its 100ms deadline: TIMEOUT. task-c (50ms) finishes before 500ms: DONE. Output sorted alphabetically.

test_cases.description: Three tasks: one barely fits, one times out, one has ample time
test_cases.expected_output:
task-a DONE
task-b TIMEOUT
task-c DONE

test_cases.description: All tasks complete within generous deadlines
All tasks exceed their very tight deadlines
